message:
[review-fix] readwrite proptest: canonicalise undirected edge orientation

`property_edgelist_round_trip` was failing on inputs where round-trip
through the edgelist text changed which endpoint of a node-pair was
inserted first into the parsed graph. `edges_ordered` then emitted (u,v)
and (v,u) for the same undirected edge, and a lex-sort by (left, right)
made them compare unequal.

Edgelist files don't preserve node order (nodes are implicit from edge
endpoints), so on undirected graphs an edge's stored orientation depends
on whichever endpoint the parser saw first. Canonicalise each edge to
(min, max) before sorting — testing "same multiset of undirected edges +
attrs" rather than the orientation per edge — to make the proptest
robust to that re-orientation. Digraph variants are unchanged because
edge orientation is meaningful there.

Reproducer caught by proptest on edges = [(0,0), (4,0), (1,7), (4,7)]:
the original graph emits (n1,n7), the parsed graph emits (n7,n1).

Verified clean run with PROPTEST_CASES=512 and the full crate suite
(140 tests passing).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
